title: Unit Fixtures, Properties, Fuzzing, Race Detection, and Leak Evidence · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds layered deterministic Go evidence suite from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of layered deterministic Go evidence suite while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, date, identity, update, malformed, entity, oversized, and extension fixtures with explicit expected evidence.
Test URL, discovery, parse, normalize, schedule, state, cursor, search, and policy functions with injected clocks and deterministic inputs.
Fuzz XML tokens, URLs, dates, cursors, state transitions, and normalization with bounded invariants and preserved minimal failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Original Fixture Matrix
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 9031 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile original fixture coverage, pure units, fuzz properties, race and leak cases, and evidence non-claims; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, date, identity, update, malformed, entity, oversized, and extension fixtures with explicit expected evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Original Fixture Matrix.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Go Test Nonclaims
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-test-nonclaims-1sp684" that uses case 5351 to reconcile original fixture coverage, pure units, fuzz properties, race and leak cases, and evidence non-claims. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
